title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's pitching staff ranks last in MLB for home runs per nine innings and 26th in team ERA.
Key players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story have struggled offensively, contributing to the Red Sox's poor performance.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, retired from the NFL after 13 seasons, announced via Instagram with a heartfelt message.
Gilmore played for six NFL teams, with notable stints at Buffalo and New England, winning a Super Bowl in 2018.
He earned five Pro Bowl selections, was 2019 Defensive Player of the Year, and finished with 32 interceptions.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle started global layoffs, but no Massachusetts layoffs reported in recent state filings.
Analysts estimate up to 30,000 layoffs, affecting 18% of Oracle's global 162,000 workforce.
Layoffs occur amid broader tech restructuring, following similar cuts by companies like Amazon and Meta.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, marking his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger previously played for the New England Patriots and spent last season with the Pittsburgh Steelers.
The former Patriots captain had 42 tackles and two interceptions in nine games with the Steelers last year.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes' truck was stolen from Logan Airport's central parking after returning from a vacation.
State Police noted similarities between Byrnes' case and another theft at the same garage weeks earlier.
Toll records suggest Byrnes' truck ended up in New Jersey; the case remains under investigation.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a 26-year-old nurse from Wakefield, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto works with students with complex needs and is inspired by their achievements despite disabilities.
Special Olympics Massachusetts promotes inclusion and empowerment, aligning with Peduto's passion to advocate for individuals with disabilities.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover Police investigate a former employee's unauthorized comment on the department's Facebook about Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
The ex-employee, who resigned amid past misconduct, allegedly posted "in other shocking news!" under a department post.
The department is conducting a thorough audit of its social media accounts and reviewing policies to prevent future breaches.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a seafood and steak restaurant in Waltham, closed after seven years due to rising costs.
The Federal, located at 74 Tower Rd., opened in 2018, providing dishes like oysters and crab cakes.
The Federal's sister property, Stazione di Federal, will stay open and honor all existing gift cards.

title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com compiled a list of 16 new spring book releases to ease readers' selection process.
The books are sorted into quadrants based on style, from fast-paced to slow-burning, and light to dark.
Readers can sign up to receive the full spring book matrix and top four recommendations via email.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express will open a two-story Centurion Lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
Massport approved a $75 million budget for the lounge shell; American Express will spend $391 million, including $10 million annually in rent.
The 20,000 square-foot lounge will feature local artwork, premium amenities, and Boston-inspired design elements reflecting the city's character.
